title: Urostachys mooreanus
intro: Urostachys mooreanus, also known as Urostachys, is a species of terrestrial, non-vascular, and perennial plant in the genus Urostachys of the family Lycopodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Urostachys mooreanus include lignans, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties. Urostachys is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, although its use may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.

Urostachys mooreanus is a rare and endemic species found primarily in the southwestern region of Western Australia.
